summary: Fix LLM judge model ID and increase eval timeouts for LLM scorer runs
context: Running eval suite with TWINING_EVAL_JUDGE=1 for the first time revealed two issues: (1) hardcoded model ID claude-haiku-4-5-20250929 no longer exists (404), (2) default 30s vitest timeout insufficient for k=3 consensus API calls, especially on transcript fixtures with 79 tool calls.
rationale: Updated model to claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 (current Haiku). Increased eval timeout to 60s (synthetic — most scenarios need only 2-8s per LLM call) and transcript timeout to 120s (real transcripts have many more tool calls requiring many more judge API calls).

alternatives.option: Use env var TWINING_EVAL_MODEL to override at runtime instead of fixing default
alternatives.reason_rejected: The default should work out of the box — requiring env var override for basic functionality is poor DX

alternatives.option: Set timeout to 300s for both configs
alternatives.reason_rejected: Overly generous timeout masks real performance issues — 60s/120s are calibrated to observed API response times with margin
